title: Cenostigma nordestinum
taxonomy: Cenostigma nordestinum is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Brazil and is a type of legume. The classification of Cenostigma nordestinum is further divided into the genus Cenostigma and the subfamily Caesalpinioideae. Within the subfamily Caesalpinioideae, Cenostigma nordestinum is classified as part of the tribe Caesalpinieae. This classification helps to understand the plant's evolutionary relationships and its place within the larger family of Fabaceae.
uses: Cenostigma nordestinum is a plant species, but information on its medicinal uses is limited. benefits: Cenostigma nordestinum, a plant native to the northeastern region of Brazil, has been found to possess several health benefits. preparations: Cenostigma nordestinum is a species of plant used in traditional medicine, and herbal preparations of this plant are commonly used in folk remedies, particularly in South America. The roots and leaves of Cenostigma nordestinum are often dried and ground into a powder for use in teas, infusions, and topical applications.
